title: John Rawls' Theory of Justice: Fairness, Equality and Differences
content: John Rawls' theory ofjustice, famously articulated in his landmark work "A Theory ofJustice" (1971), remains one of the most significant contributions to modern political philosophy. By introducing the concept of "justiceasfairness," Rawls sought to establish a normative framework for structuring a justsociety. His reliance on the original position and the veil of ignorance as ...
